title: Why Go Solar in Australia
description: Electricity prices, STC rebates and NEM feed-in tariffs make 2026 the right time to switch.

hero.title: Why Go Solar in Australia
hero.lede: Electricity prices, STC rebates and NEM feed-in tariffs make 2026 the right time to switch.

prose: Australia has the highest residential solar penetration in the world — and for good reason. Abundant sunshine, falling equipment costs and federal STC rebates create compelling economics for homeowners and businesses.
The average 6.6 kW system saves $1,200–$2,400 AUD per year depending on self-consumption, state irradiance and electricity retailer.
Each kW of solar avoids approximately 1.5 tonnes of CO₂ annually. An 8 kW system offsets the equivalent of planting 150 trees per year.
With batteries and EV chargers, you control more of your energy supply — reducing exposure to NEM price volatility and grid outages.
STCs remain available in 2026, with state battery incentives in NSW, Victoria, SA and ACT stacking for eligible households.
